product.name: ArgusV
product.tagline: AI-Native Surveillance Platform with Video RAG Chat
product.description: ArgusV is an enterprise-grade, AI-native Network Video Recorder (NVR) and surveillance platform built on FastAPI and asyncio. Unlike Frigate (SQLite + monolithic Python process), ArgusV uses PostgreSQL, Redis, and a clean async pipeline architecture with first-class Video RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) chat, semantic video search, and multi-channel notification orchestration.

frigate.web_framework: Custom HTTP (port 5000 internal) + NGINX reverse proxy (port 8971)
frigate.camera_pipeline: FFmpeg per camera → /dev/shm → detector processes → enrichment processes
frigate.ai_inference: TFLite / ONNX / OpenVINO / TensorRT on dedicated detector processes
frigate.embeddings: CLIP (Jina AI V1/V2) stored in SQLite binary column

argusv.camera_pipeline: FrameBuffer → MotionGate → YOLO+ByteTrack → ZoneMatcher → DwellTracker → asyncio queues
argusv.ai_inference: YOLOv8 (ultralytics) + ByteTrack in edge_worker thread, VLM in async pipeline
argusv.frontend: Vanilla JS HTML5 (4 pages: dashboard, incidents, recordings, zones)
argusv.embeddings: CLIP EmbeddingManager (planned, TODO VLM-08) → pgvector or Qdrant
differentiators: Video RAG Chat (natural language query over video history)

primary_users.needs: Real-time alerts, incident review, video clip access, natural language video search
Dashboard overview, incident reports, zone configuration, notification routing
Camera management, storage configuration, user management, system health
Fast incident triage, video replay, threat classification, escalation workflows

secondary_users.needs: Simple setup, mobile notifications, easy playback
Docker deployment, metrics, log access, scalability

epics.name: Core Detection Pipeline
epics.description: Complete the full detection pipeline from camera ingestion to threat classification

features.title: Multi-Camera YOLO Object Detection
features.description: YOLOv8 inference with ByteTrack tracking per camera via edge_worker threads
acceptance_criteria: Detect: person, car, truck, motorcycle, bicycle, bus, dog, cat (configurable)
ByteTrack assigns persistent track IDs across frames
Processes RTSP streams from MediaMTX re-broadcaster

features.title: Motion Gating
features.description: Pre-filter YOLO inference with motion detection to reduce compute cost
acceptance_criteria: Skip YOLO inference on frames with no motion
Motion debug overlay available via API

features.title: Zone-Based Detection (Polygon Matching)
features.description: Associate detections with configured security zones using Shapely polygons
acceptance_criteria: Polygon zones stored in PostgreSQL (JSONB coords)
Shapely Point-in-Polygon for bounding box bottom-center
Detections outside all zones still recorded (no-zone events)
Zone inertia: require N consecutive frames before trigger (configurable)

features.title: Loitering / Dwell Detection
features.description: Track how long an object remains in a zone and trigger alerts on threshold breach
acceptance_criteria: LOITERING event emitted when threshold exceeded
Dwell time included in DetectionEvent metadata

features.title: Stationary Object Tracking
features.description: Reduce re-detection cadence for objects that haven't moved
acceptance_criteria: After N frames (configurable), only re-detect stationary objects every M frames

features.title: Speed Estimation
features.description: Estimate object speed through calibrated 4-point zones
acceptance_criteria: 4-point homography calibration per zone (real-world distance mapping)
Speed calculated in km/h or mph
Speed threshold alerts configurable per zone

features.title: Audio Event Detection
features.description: Detect audio events (barking, screaming, glass breaking, gunshots) from camera streams
acceptance_criteria: Configurable event types to listen for per camera
Audio events stored in Detection table with audio_type label

epics.name: VLM Threat Analysis (Video RAG Core)
epics.description: AI-powered visual threat analysis using Vision-Language Models with tiered inference strategy

features.title: Tiered VLM Inference (GPT-4o-mini → GPT-4o)
features.description: Cost-optimized two-stage VLM pipeline: cheap triage model first, expensive full model only if needed
acceptance_criteria: Stage 2: GPT-4o full analysis (if triage says YES)
Returns: {threat_level, is_threat, summary, recommended_action, confidence, objects_detected}

features.title: Multi-Provider VLM Support
features.description: Support multiple GenAI providers with runtime switching

features.title: Review Summaries with Structured Output
features.description: GenAI generates structured review summaries with danger classification (like Frigate 0.17)
acceptance_criteria: Review segment summary generated for incident groupings

features.title: Object Descriptions (Per Detection)
features.description: Per-object VLM descriptions with natural language descriptions of what was detected
acceptance_criteria: VLM generates description for each significant detection
Description used as input to CLIP text encoder for semantic search
Required zones filter before triggering description

features.title: Video RAG Chat Interface
features.description: Natural language chat interface to query video history using RAG over CLIP embeddings + VLM descriptions
acceptance_criteria: User sends natural language query: 'show me all people who entered Zone A between 2-4pm'
System: (1) encode query as CLIP embedding, (2) vector search pgvector, (3) retrieve top-K matching detections, (4) pass context + frames to VLM, (5) generate grounded response
Response includes: text answer + list of matched detection IDs with timestamps + thumbnail URLs
Filter by: camera, zone, time range, object class
Response cites specific incidents by ID with deep-links

features.title: Semantic Search (Text + Image Query)
features.description: CLIP-powered semantic search over all indexed detection thumbnails
acceptance_criteria: CLIP text embedding generated for query
pgvector cosine similarity search on Detection.embedding column
Semantic search triggers: automate actions when similarity exceeds threshold
Minimum: 8GB RAM + AVX CPU; recommended: 16GB + GPU
Models: Jina AI CLIP V1 (small/large) or CLIP V2 (multilingual)

features.title: CLIP Embeddings Pipeline
features.description: Generate and store CLIP embeddings for all detection thumbnails
acceptance_criteria: EmbeddingManager generates CLIP embeddings on START events
Embeddings stored in pgvector column on Detection table (vector(512))
Support multiple embedding models via config (CLIP_MODEL env var)

features.title: Face Recognition
features.description: Identify known persons and apply sub-labels to detections
acceptance_criteria: Named person sub-labels applied to Detection.sub_label
Training via UI: upload 5-100 images per person
Unknown faces stored for later labeling

features.title: License Plate Recognition (LPR)
features.description: Detect and read license plates from vehicles
acceptance_criteria: YOLOv9 plate detector + PaddleOCR character recognition
Known plates watchlist with configurable alerts
Fuzzy matching for OCR error tolerance (Levenshtein distance)

epics.name: Recording & Playback System
epics.description: Full NVR recording pipeline with HLS segments, MinIO storage, timeline scrubbing, and clip export

features.title: HLS Segment Recording
features.description: FFmpeg records camera streams to HLS segments stored in MinIO
acceptance_criteria: Segments written to /tmp/cache first, then uploaded to MinIO
Segment metadata written to PostgreSQL Segment table
Pre-capture buffer: N seconds before detection (default 10s)
Post-capture buffer: N seconds after detection ends (default 30s)

features.title: HLS Playlist Generation & Streaming
features.description: Generate M3U8 playlists for segment-level playback via MediaMTX
acceptance_criteria: Segments served from MinIO pre-signed URLs

features.title: Detection Timeline Markers
features.description: Overlay detection events on recording timeline for fast navigation
acceptance_criteria: Each marker: {timestamp, threat_level, object_class, zone, thumbnail_url}
Timeline rendered in recordings.html as colored markers
Click marker → seek player to that timestamp
Color coding: HIGH=red, MEDIUM=orange, LOW=yellow, motion=gray

features.title: Clip Export
features.description: Generate and export video clips from incidents or arbitrary time ranges
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/exports/{id}/download → presigned MinIO URL for clip download
Incident replay shortcut: GET /api/incidents/{id}/replay → incident clip

features.title: Snapshot & Thumbnail System
features.description: Best-frame snapshot extraction for each detection event
acceptance_criteria: snapshot_worker.py extracts best frame per detection
Best-frame algorithm: highest confidence + largest bbox + face/plate priority
Annotated JPEG (bbox + label overlay) stored in MinIO: snapshots/<camera>/<event_id>.jpg
Configurable: timestamp overlay, bbox color, crop to bbox, height
GET /api/events/{id}/snapshot → redirect to MinIO presigned URL
MQTT publish: argusv/<camera>/snapshot (JPEG base64) on new detection

features.title: Preview Video Generation
features.description: Generate low-bitrate preview videos for review queue (bandwidth-efficient)
acceptance_criteria: After detection group ends, generate 480p preview clip
Review queue uses previews (not full-res) for thumbnail grid

features.title: Retention Policy & Storage Cleanup
features.description: Automated segment deletion based on configurable retention rules
acceptance_criteria: Tiered retention: continuous=3d, motion=7d, detection=30d, alert=90d
Storage threshold: alert when < 20% free space, auto-delete oldest when < 10%
MinIO object deletion + PostgreSQL Segment table cleanup
sync_recordings: reconcile DB entries with MinIO objects

epics.name: Review & Incident Triage System
epics.description: Alert triage queue with grouped detection review, severity tiers, and incident lifecycle management

features.title: Incident Grouping & Review Queue
features.description: Bundle overlapping detections into review segments with Alert/Detection severity tiers
acceptance_criteria: ReviewMaintainer groups detections within configurable time window (default: 5 minutes)
Two severity tiers: ALERT (HIGH threat = person/vehicle in restricted zone) and DETECTION (everything else)
ReviewSegment stored in PostgreSQL with start_time, end_time, severity, thumb_path
GET /api/review → filterable list (camera, severity, date, object, zone, status)
Unreviewed alert count badge on dashboard

features.title: Incident Detail View
features.description: Full incident details with timeline of sub-detections, VLM summaries, and associated clips
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/incidents/{id} → full incident with all Detection children
Timeline of sub-detections within incident window
VLM summary per detection + overall incident summary
Object lifecycle view: track appearance → loitering → departure

features.title: Incident Lifecycle Management
features.description: Resolve, annotate, dismiss, and escalate incidents
acceptance_criteria: Incident status: OPEN (default), RESOLVED (operator confirmed + handled), DISMISSED (false positive), ESCALATED (sent to external system)
Auto-resolve after N hours if no further activity (configurable)
Retain-indefinitely flag: never auto-delete associated recordings

features.title: Motion Review Mode
features.description: Review timeline showing motion activity periods for potentially missed events
acceptance_criteria: Returns motion intensity timeline (% motion per interval)
UI: scrub timeline to review motion periods not triggering YOLO
Threshold: show motion segments above configurable threshold

epics.name: Zone & Mask Configuration
epics.description: Full zone and mask management with polygon editor UI

features.title: Zone CRUD API
features.description: Full REST API for zone management
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/zones → create zone {name, camera_id, polygon_coords, dwell_threshold_sec, active, alert_objects, detection_objects}
Polygon stored as relative coordinates (0.0-1.0) in JSONB
Dynamic reload without restart (runtime zone updates)

features.title: Polygon Editor UI
features.description: Browser-based polygon drawing tool for zones and masks
acceptance_criteria: Camera snapshot as background in zones.html
Object class filter checkboxes per zone

features.title: Motion Masks
features.description: Exclude areas from motion detection
acceptance_criteria: Motion mask polygons stored per camera in Camera.motion_masks JSONB
Applied before motion detection step in MotionGate
UI: same polygon editor used for zones, toggle mode to 'mask'

features.title: Object Filter Masks
features.description: Exclude detection areas (ignore bbox bottom-center in mask region)
acceptance_criteria: Applied after YOLO inference, before zone matching
Configurable per object class (e.g., ignore cars in parking lot)

epics.name: Notification & Alert Routing System
epics.description: Multi-channel notification dispatcher with routing rules, cooldowns, and rich media support

features.title: WebSocket Live Alert Feed
features.description: Real-time alert broadcast to dashboard via WebSocket
acceptance_criteria: ConnectionManager handles multiple concurrent dashboard clients
fan_out_loop() reads bus.alerts_ws and broadcasts to all connections
Event types: fast_alert, vlm_update, incident_created, system_health
Reconnect with exponential backoff in dashboard.html JS client

features.title: Slack Integration
features.description: Slack channel notifications with threat level, zone, summary, and thumbnail
acceptance_criteria: Message format: emoji + threat_level + camera + zone + object_class + VLM summary
Rate limiting: RATE_LIMIT_TTL cooldown per camera
Error handling: retry on 429, log on failure

features.title: WebPush Browser Notifications
features.description: VAPID-based web push notifications for browsers and PWA
acceptance_criteria: PWA manifest.json for home screen installability

features.title: MQTT Alert Publishing
features.description: Full MQTT topic tree for integration with Home Assistant, Node-RED, and custom systems
acceptance_criteria: MQTT broker connection (MQTT_HOST, MQTT_PORT, MQTT_USER, MQTT_PASS, TLS)
argusv/<camera>/<object>/snapshot → JPEG base64 on new detection

features.title: Webhook Notifications
features.description: HTTP webhook delivery to custom endpoints
acceptance_criteria: Configurable URL, headers, method (POST/PUT), payload template
Delivery: async HTTP POST with configurable retry (3x exponential backoff)
Signature header: HMAC-SHA256 of payload for verification

features.title: Notification Rule Engine
features.description: Flexible routing rules: which alerts go to which channels
acceptance_criteria: Match logic: camera + zone + severity → list of channels
Channel configs: slack_channel, webhook_url, push_all, mqtt_topic
Rate limit: max N alerts per M minutes per camera

features.title: Email Notifications
features.description: SMTP email alerts with incident summary and thumbnail
acceptance_criteria: SMTP configuration: SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, SMTP_FROM
HTML email template: thumbnail + threat summary + deep-link to incident

epics.name: PTZ & Camera Actuation
epics.description: Pan-Tilt-Zoom control and auto-tracking for ONVIF-compatible cameras

features.title: ONVIF PTZ Control
features.description: ONVIF-based PTZ commands via REST API and MQTT
acceptance_criteria: ONVIF camera discovery and profile enumeration
Commands: move (pan/tilt/zoom relative+absolute), stop, preset-goto, preset-save
REST: POST /api/cameras/{id}/ptz {action, preset?, pan?, tilt?, zoom?}

features.title: Auto-Tracking
features.description: Automatically track moving objects by adjusting PTZ to keep object centered
acceptance_criteria: Continuously adjusts pan/tilt to center tracked object bbox
Required zones to trigger autotracking (prevent tracking outside ROI)
Return-to-home-preset on object loss after timeout
Zoom-in on object (absolute or relative zoom)
Startup calibration: measure motor speed, write movement_weights to config

features.title: MQTT Actuation (Siren / Relay)
features.description: Trigger physical devices (sirens, lights, relays) via MQTT on threat detection
acceptance_criteria: HIGH threats → publish to MQTT siren topic
Configurable MQTT topic + payload per rule
Manual trigger: POST /api/actuate/{device} {action: on|off|pulse, duration: N}
Device registry in config: [[device_name, mqtt_topic, on_payload, off_payload]]

epics.name: Live View & Streaming
epics.description: Multi-protocol live camera viewing with birdseye composite view

features.title: HLS Live Streaming
features.description: Low-latency HLS live stream for all cameras via MediaMTX
acceptance_criteria: MediaMTX re-broadcasts RTSP as HLS at /api/cameras/{id}/live.m3u8
Dashboard camera grid shows live thumbnails (1fps smart mode)
Latency target: < 5 seconds HLS, < 1 second WebRTC

features.title: WebRTC Live Streaming
features.description: Sub-second latency WebRTC live view via MediaMTX WebRTC
acceptance_criteria: Automatic protocol fallback: WebRTC → HLS → JPEG snapshot

features.title: Birdseye Composite View
features.description: Multi-camera composite overview with configurable layout
acceptance_criteria: BirdsEyeRenderer in output/birdseye.py generates composite frame
Configurable modes: continuous, motion (30s window), objects (30s window)
Max cameras limit with cooldown switching

epics.name: Camera Management
epics.description: Full camera lifecycle management: add, configure, monitor, enable/disable

features.title: Camera CRUD API
features.description: Full REST API for camera management
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/cameras → list all cameras with status
POST /api/cameras → add camera {name, rtsp_url, detect_fps, enabled}

features.title: Camera Health Monitoring
features.description: Watchdog for camera connectivity and stream health
acceptance_criteria: Alert if camera offline > N minutes
Queue depth monitoring: alert if vlm_requests backlog > threshold
GET /api/health → {cameras: {id: {status, fps, queue_depth}}, system: {cpu, ram, gpu}}

features.title: Add Camera Wizard
features.description: Step-by-step UI wizard for adding and configuring new cameras
acceptance_criteria: Step 1: Enter RTSP URL → auto-discover stream properties
Step 2: Set name, detect FPS, recording mode
Step 3: Preview live snapshot from camera
Review and Save → camera starts automatically

epics.name: Authentication & Authorization
epics.description: JWT-based auth with role-based access control

features.title: JWT Authentication
features.description: Secure JWT-based login with PBKDF2 password hashing
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/login → {username, password} → JWT access token + refresh token
JWT secret: 64+ char cryptographic random (ARGUSV_JWT_SECRET env var)
Rate limiting: brute force protection on /api/login

features.title: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Admin and Viewer roles with camera-level restrictions
acceptance_criteria: Roles: admin (full access), viewer (read-only), operator (triage + resolve only)
Admin: camera CRUD, zone CRUD, user management, system config
Viewer: view live, view incidents, view recordings (configurable per camera)
Operator: all viewer + resolve/dismiss incidents + acknowledge alerts

features.title: Proxy Authentication Support
features.description: Support SSO via reverse proxy auth headers (Authelia, Authentik, Keycloak)
acceptance_criteria: Trust X-Remote-User and X-Remote-Groups headers from trusted proxy
Auto-provision user on first login if proxy auth enabled
Role mapping from proxy group headers

epics.name: Statistics & Observability
epics.description: System metrics, detection statistics, and health dashboards

features.title: System Statistics API
features.description: Real-time system metrics endpoint
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/stats → {detectors: {fps, inference_ms}, cameras: {fps, detection_fps, process_fps}, system: {uptime, cpu_pct, ram_pct, gpu_pct, disk_free_pct}, queues: {vlm_backlog, ws_clients}}
Historical stats stored in Redis (time-series, 24h retention)

features.title: Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
features.description: Prometheus-compatible /metrics endpoint for Grafana integration
acceptance_criteria: Metrics: argusv_detections_total{camera, object, zone, threat_level}, argusv_vlm_inference_seconds{provider, model}, argusv_camera_fps{camera}, argusv_incident_count{status}, argusv_queue_depth{queue}
Standard process metrics: CPU, RAM, GC, file descriptors

features.title: Detection Analytics
features.description: Aggregated detection statistics and trend analysis
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/events/summary → count by {camera, label, zone, hour/day/week}
Peak activity charts by hour-of-day per camera

epics.name: Configuration Management
epics.description: Runtime configuration management with UI settings panel

features.title: Configuration API
features.description: REST API to read and update system configuration at runtime
acceptance_criteria: Dynamic reload without restart for: zones, masks, notification rules, thresholds
Requires restart for: camera additions, recording format changes, model changes
Config versioning: store last 5 config snapshots in PostgreSQL

features.title: Settings UI Panel
features.description: Web-based settings UI for all configurable parameters
acceptance_criteria: Sections: General, Cameras, Detection, Recording, Notifications, AI/VLM, Auth, Storage
Per-camera motion threshold tuning with live preview
VLM provider selector with model dropdown

epics.name: Database & Storage Infrastructure
epics.description: PostgreSQL schema, migrations, and MinIO object storage integration

features.title: PostgreSQL Schema (Complete)
features.description: Complete database schema with all models and proper migrations
acceptance_criteria: All models implemented: Camera, Segment, Zone, Rule, Detection, Incident, NotificationRule, RagConfig, User, Export, ReviewSegment, Trigger
Detection.embedding: vector(512) column via pgvector extension
All indexes: camera_ts, segment, incident, detection timestamps
Alembic migrations: versioned, reversible, auto-generated from models
Connection pooling: asyncpg for async workers, psycopg2 for sync endpoints
Database health check: GET /api/health includes DB connectivity

features.title: pgvector Extension & Semantic Index
features.description: PostgreSQL pgvector for CLIP embedding storage and cosine similarity search
acceptance_criteria: Detection.embedding: vector(512) with IVFFlat index (lists=100, probes=10)
Cosine similarity query: SELECT id, 1 - (embedding <=> query_vec) AS score FROM detections ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 50

features.title: MinIO Object Storage Integration
features.description: S3-compatible object storage for all media files
acceptance_criteria: MinIO client initialized from MINIO_ENDPOINT, MINIO_ACCESS_KEY, MINIO_SECRET_KEY
Presigned URLs for client-side download (TTL: 1h default)
Async upload using aioboto3 or minio-py async

endpoints.params: q, camera, from, to, limit, threshold

video_rag_chat_specification.description: ArgusV's primary differentiator: natural language querying over video surveillance history

architecture.step1: User submits query via POST /api/chat/query or WebSocket /ws/chat
architecture.step2: Query preprocessed: extract intent, filters (camera, zone, time range, object class)
architecture.step3: CLIP text encoder generates 512-dim embedding for query
architecture.step4: pgvector cosine similarity search over detections with matching filters
architecture.step5: Top-K (default 20) matching detections retrieved with metadata
architecture.step6: Context assembled: detection metadata + thumbnail URLs + VLM descriptions
architecture.step7: VLM called with query + context: generate grounded natural language response
architecture.step8: Response streamed back via WebSocket with cited detection IDs
architecture.step9: Chat session persisted to PostgreSQL chat_sessions table

example_queries: Show me all people who entered Zone A between 2pm and 4pm today
Were there any suspicious vehicles in the parking lot last night?
Find detections similar to this image (image upload)
How many people passed through the main entrance yesterday?
Show me all HIGH threat incidents from this week
Did anyone loiter near the server room for more than 5 minutes?
What was the most active camera yesterday evening?
Find all incidents involving red vehicles
Show me all times the person in blue jacket appeared on camera 3

response_format.answer: Natural language response grounded in retrieved detections

semantic_triggers.description: Automate actions when new detections match a stored semantic query
semantic_triggers.example: Alert when detected object is similar to stored 'person with backpack near server room' embedding

docker_services.name: redis
docker_services.description: Cache, pub/sub, rate limiting, session store

docker_services.name: nginx
docker_services.description: TLS termination + reverse proxy (optional, for production)

phase_1_mvp.description: Core detection → review → notification pipeline fully working
items: EPIC-03 REC-01: HLS recording to MinIO (recording_worker complete)
EPIC-05 ZONE-01: Zone CRUD API complete
EPIC-05 ZONE-02: Polygon editor UI complete

phase_2_ai.description: Full AI feature set: semantic search + Video RAG chat + CLIP embeddings
items: EPIC-02 VLM-07: CLIP embedding pipeline + pgvector
EPIC-02 VLM-05: Video RAG Chat (REST + WebSocket)

phase_3_enterprise.description: Enterprise features: face recognition, LPR, analytics, Prometheus

performance: Detection pipeline latency: < 500ms from frame capture to DetectionEvent on bus
API response time: < 200ms for list endpoints (p95)
WebSocket alert delivery: < 1s from detection to dashboard display
Semantic search: < 2s for text query across 1M embeddings (pgvector IVFFlat)
Video RAG chat: first token < 3s, streaming response < 30s
Simultaneous cameras: 8+ cameras at 5fps detection each on recommended hardware
reliability: Camera reconnect: automatic retry with exponential backoff on stream loss
Pipeline recovery: workers restart automatically on crash (asyncio supervision)
Recording durability: segment uploaded to MinIO before acknowledging pipeline
Notification delivery: retry 3x with exponential backoff on failure
Database connections: connection pool with health checks, automatic reconnect
security: JWT tokens rotate on password change
MinIO presigned URLs expire after 1 hour
RTSP credentials never exposed via API (masked in /api/cameras)
SQL injection prevention via SQLAlchemy ORM (parameterized queries only)
Audit log: all authentication events and destructive actions logged
scalability: Horizontal scaling: multiple argusv instances behind load balancer (shared PostgreSQL + Redis + MinIO)
Camera sharding: distribute cameras across multiple argusv instances
VLM worker scaling: VLM_MAX_WORKERS controls concurrent inference
pgvector scales to 10M+ embeddings with IVFFlat index
MinIO cluster: S3-compatible, scales to petabytes

delivery_scan.method: Cross-checked workbook task groupings against the implemented FastAPI routes, background workers, static UIs, SQLAlchemy models, and tests under argusv/src, argusv/static, and argusv/tests.
implemented_highlights: DET-01 through DET-04 are implemented in src/workers/edge_worker.py with YOLO tracking, motion gating, zone matching, and dwell/loiter lifecycle events.
ZONE-01 and the API-10/API-11 backend work are implemented in src/api/routes/zones.py with CRUD, polygon validation, and Redis hot-reload publishing.
API-12 through API-15 and DNVR replay/timeline support are implemented in src/api/routes/recordings.py and wired into static/recordings.html.
API-16 and API-17 are implemented in src/api/routes/incidents.py and exposed in the incidents review UI at static/incidents.html.
AUTH-01, AUTH-02, AUTH-06, and AUTH-07 have a working baseline in src/api/routes/auth.py, src/auth/jwt_handler.py, and src/api/routes/users.py.
partial_or_local_only: REC-01 through REC-07 are only partially complete: the recorder, playlist, replay, snapshots, and cleanup flows exist, but they currently use local filesystem paths instead of real MinIO-backed object storage.

VLM-06 and VLM-07 have a working baseline via src/api/routes/rag.py and src/workers/rag_worker.py, but they use sentence-transformer text embeddings, not full CLIP/image search, and they do not provide reindex or chat workflows.
CFG-01 has runtime config and notification-rule APIs in src/api/routes/configuration.py, but versioning, restore flows, and a settings UI are still missing.
STATS-01 has a collector in src/stats/emitter.py, but /api/stats and /metrics are not currently wired into the FastAPI app.
stubbed_or_missing: PTZ control and MQTT actuation remain scaffolding in src/ptz/autotrack.py and src/workers/actuation_worker.py.
The review queue/grouping system is not operational yet; src/review/maintainer.py is still TODO-based scaffolding and is not started by api/server.py.
WATCH-02, WATCH-03, WATCH-05, WATCH-07, and WATCH-08 are still incomplete; only queue-depth monitoring has real logic in src/workers/watchdog_worker.py.
WebPush, webhook, email, and real MQTT outbound delivery paths remain unimplemented or stubbed.
verification_notes: The repository has targeted tests for auth, zones, queue stats, and segment linkage, but local python -m pytest -q did not complete in this environment because the ad-hoc Python runtime was missing pgvector and tests/test_api.py is still commented out.

frigate_feature_parity_matrix.description: Status of ArgusV feature parity with Frigate NVR 0.17.0
